description: 63M, CKD 3a (eGFR 52), albuminuria A1 (ACR 22 mg/g), no diabetes. Moderate CKD without albuminuria or diabetes narrows scope to monitoring + statin.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1A: adults aged ≥50 with CKD G3a-G5 not on dialysis should receive moderate-intensity statin regardless of LDL-C. Patient is 63 with eGFR 52 (G3a) and no current statin. KDIGO specifically recommends moderate-intensity over high-intensity in CKD due to altered pharmacokinetics.

actions.label: Ensure annual eGFR and urine ACR monitoring
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: all patients with CKD should have at least annual eGFR and albuminuria monitoring. G3a-A1 is low risk on the KDIGO heatmap; annual monitoring is the minimum frequency.

actions.label: Comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment
actions.rationale: CKD is an independent cardiovascular risk factor. Beyond statin, assess BP control, smoking status, and metabolic parameters. Patient's BP 132/78 may warrant treatment per KDIGO BP targets (<120 systolic when tolerated).

contraindications.label: High-intensity statin therapy
contraindications.rationale: KDIGO specifically recommends moderate-intensity in CKD G3-G5 due to altered pharmacokinetics and increased myopathy risk. High-intensity would be inappropriate.

contraindications.label: SGLT2 inhibitor initiation
contraindications.rationale: Patient has no diabetes and ACR 22 mg/g (below 200 mg/g threshold). KDIGO SGLT2 recommendation does not apply without T2DM or significant albuminuria.
